audits.name: youtube-transcript
findings: No shared memory reads — skill operates as an isolated tool
No shared memory writes — results don't propagate to learnings/decisions
No cross-skill references
Does NOT follow PLAN→WORK→ASSESS→COMPOUND — it's a linear pipeline
No budget awareness
Isolated skills are acceptable if the use case is standalone, but this skill could record download failures to learnings.md
recommendations: Add: read .claude/memory/checkpoint.md to understand project context (already in instructions: 'check memory/checkpoint for context' but not using the skill's allowed-tools pattern)
Add: write to .claude/memory/learnings.md when yt-dlp workarounds are discovered (anti-pattern: MCP server broken)
Low integration is acceptable for a utility skill — score reflects intentional isolation

audits.name: autoloop
findings: Reads budget.md before starting — correct budget awareness
Writes to budget.md event log after run — correct tracking
Follows COMPOUND loop: iterate → measure → keep/revert → log
Does NOT reference shared learnings.md for pre-run context
No cross-skill references (no /critic invocation after optimization)
Missing: doesn't suggest running /critic after loop completes
recommendations: Add: read .claude/memory/learnings.md at start to apply known patterns to optimization
Add: after loop completes, suggest '/critic <target>' for quality validation
Add: write discovered optimization patterns to learnings.md on success

audits.name: budget
findings: Core purpose IS memory management — reads and writes budget.md
Budget awareness is the skill itself — maximum integration
References orchestrator pattern (auto-invoked by orchestrator)
Circuit breakers are well-defined and system-integrated
COMPOUND loop not applicable — this is a utility tracker, not a task executor
recommendations: No changes needed — integration is the core function

audits.name: checkpoint
findings: Reads state.md, budget.md, decisions.md, learnings.md, implementation-plan.md — comprehensive
Writes checkpoint.md — primary output is shared memory
Referenced by orchestrate (auto-trigger on context health check)
Reads budget state as part of checkpoint — correct
COMPOUND loop not applicable — this is a session boundary tool
Exemplary integration: reads everything, synthesizes into resumable state
recommendations: No changes needed — gold standard for memory integration

audits.name: dependency-audit
findings: Reads news.md to cross-reference /watch findings — good
Writes to learnings.md, decisions.md, news.md after completion — correct
Cross-references /watch and /incident-runbook in description — good
No budget awareness — skill doesn't check budget before web searches
COMPOUND loop partially followed: work → record findings, but no self-check
recommendations: Add budget awareness: WebSearch can be expensive in volume — check budget tier before running full audit
The 'After Completion' section is good — no changes there

audits.name: incident-runbook
findings: Reads runbook.md (skill-local file) — but NOT shared memory
Tells user to update runbook but CANNOT write (correctly disallowed)
No cross-skill references beyond suggesting 'tell user to add to runbook'
Intentionally isolated: fast diagnosis, no overhead
The 'Step 5: Update runbook' is a hand-off instruction, not actual memory write
Could benefit from reading learnings.md for project-specific patterns
recommendations: Add: read .claude/memory/learnings.md — project-specific anti-patterns could help diagnosis
The no-write design is correct for a first-responder — don't change that
Consider adding Write permission scoped to runbook.md only (not general Write) — OR keep current design and accept low integration score

audits.name: orchestrate
findings: Reads state.md, decisions.md, learnings.md, budget.md, checkpoint.md, news.md — most comprehensive reader
Writes to state.md, budget.md via delegation to scribe/budget skills
References all major skills: scout, critic, scribe, budget, checkpoint, skill-generator, harness
Follows PLAN→WORK→ASSESS→COMPOUND explicitly in Phase structure
Budget awareness is central: Phase 0 checks budget, gates every subtask
Context health check is sophisticated — multi-signal scoring
Gold standard for full system integration
recommendations: No changes needed — orchestrate is the reference implementation for integration

audits.name: project-init
findings: Writes memory template files (state.md, decisions.md, etc.) — but creates templates, not system entries
References orchestrate and scout in output instructions
Does not read existing shared memory (correct — new project has none)
No budget awareness — acceptable for a one-time setup operation
COMPOUND loop not applicable — this is a bootstrap tool
Low integration is by design: initializer creates the system, doesn't use it
recommendations: No changes needed — low integration is correct for a bootstrap skill

audits.name: scout
findings: Reads learnings.md at start — correct: 'apply known patterns to speed up exploration'
Writes to state.md (scout findings) and budget.md (agent spawn counter) — correct
Budget awareness: checks budget.md for tier before choosing exploration depth — excellent
Cross-reference: mentions orchestrate implicitly (part of orchestrate workflow)
After exploration: updates state.md AND notes new patterns for learnings.md
COMPOUND loop partially: PLAN (tier check) → WORK (explore) → missing ASSESS
recommendations: Add: explicit ASSESS step — 'after scouting, validate findings make sense before reporting'
The learnings.md read-at-start pattern is the right approach — good model for other skills

audits.name: scribe
findings: Core purpose IS reading and writing shared memory — state.md, decisions.md, learnings.md, budget.md
Read-before-write pattern explicitly stated: 'always read the target file first' — correct
Auto-invoked by orchestrate — well-integrated into workflow
Maintenance procedure is well-defined with thresholds
No budget awareness — appropriate: scribe is cheap (haiku, minimal turns)
COMPOUND loop not applicable — scribe IS the compound recorder
recommendations: No changes needed — scribe's role is the integration layer itself

audits.name: skill-generator
findings: Reads learnings.md and skill-generator/LEARNINGS.md before starting — good
Writes to learnings.md, decisions.md after completion — correct COMPOUND pattern
References orchestrate, scout, critic, scribe in System Integration section
Follows partial COMPOUND: read patterns → create → record learnings
No budget awareness — should check budget before spawning (but Agent is disallowed, so less critical)
Has skill-specific LEARNINGS.md — good specialization pattern
recommendations: Add budget awareness for the 'improve-all' / 'audit' flow — this runs across many files
The LEARNINGS.md in skill directory is a good pattern — consider documenting it

audits.name: verify
findings: Reads state.md for task context — correct
Does NOT write to shared memory after completion — missed opportunity
Verify is read+execute: results should be logged to state.md
No budget awareness despite potentially expensive Agent use
Cross-reference to critic is implicit (complementary tools)
COMPOUND loop missing: no ASSESS/COMPOUND after verification
recommendations: Add: write verification result to .claude/memory/state.md after completion
Add: if FAIL, write to .claude/memory/learnings.md as an anti-pattern finding
Add budget awareness: check budget.md before spawning agents
Current score is low due to no memory writes — this is a fixable gap

audits.name: watch
findings: Reads news.md and learnings.md before scanning — correct
Reads CLAUDE.md for project context — correct
Writes to news.md, learnings.md, state.md after scan — comprehensive
Cost control section includes token estimates and model guidance — budget-aware
References /dependency-audit explicitly — good cross-skill integration
Follows COMPOUND: scan → filter → record → suggest actions
7-day cadence trigger via news.md check is a good time-based integration
recommendations: No major issues
Consider: write scan results to decisions.md when action items are found (not just news.md)

audits.name: critic
findings: Reads state.md and learnings.md at start — correct
Writes to budget.md (critic counter), state.md (review note), learnings.md (anti-patterns) — comprehensive
Budget awareness: checks budget.md, increments counter, enforces tier limit — excellent
References orchestrate (conductor triggers critic), scribe (logging pattern)
Follows COMPOUND: review → record anti-patterns → escalate if needed
Circuit breaker implementation (2 FAILs → stop) is well-integrated
Separate session recommendation for self-review is a sophisticated meta-rule
recommendations: No changes needed — second gold standard alongside orchestrate for integration quality

audits.name: harness
findings: Reads budget.md before expensive phases — correct
Writes to state.md and decisions.md after completion — correct
References orchestrate (complementary tools), implicitly references critic (quality gate mention)
Follows COMPOUND through phase structure: each phase validates before proceeding
Phase-level validation is a stronger ASSESS mechanism than most skills
Missing: harness doesn't read learnings.md at start to apply accumulated knowledge
The .harness/ directory pattern for intermediate results is the right approach
recommendations: Add: read .claude/memory/learnings.md at Phase 0 — apply known patterns to execution
The phase validation pattern is good — consider documenting as a system-wide pattern in learnings.md
